Fix search_enhancements and read_badi_definition for real SAP systems

- Fix XML parsing to handle ADT namespace prefixes (adtcore:objectReference,
  adtcore:name, adtcore:type, adtcore:description)
- Fix BADi search objectType codes: use ENHS, ENHO, SXSD, SXCI
  (removed invalid ENHS/BADI type)
- Add fallback for non-self-closing XML tags
- Add debug logging with response size for troubleshooting
- read_badi_definition now tries multiple ADT endpoints (enhspots,
  badidefinitions, repository search) before giving up
- Improved error messages suggesting search_enhancements first

commit.message:
fix: names index template, letter routing, search icon positioning

Bug 1: /names/ page was rendering GP's default template. Added
templates/archive-names.php (search bar + A-Z nav) and wired it
into template_include via is_page('names'). Also added is_page('names')
to body_class and enqueue conditions.

Bug 2: /names/letter/a/ was still routing to the name 'Adelaide'.
Added explicit add_rewrite_rule() at init priority 1 (before CPT/
taxonomy registration at 10) with 'top' position so our rule is
evaluated first. Also called lnb_register_rewrite_rules() from
lnb_activate() before flush_rewrite_rules().

Bug 3: Search icon overlapping input text. Added position:relative
!important on .lnb-search-wrap, box-sizing:border-box !important and
padding !important on the input, and display:inline-flex + line-height:0
on the icon span. Added a GP-specific input override to prevent the
theme resetting our padding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: bouton Focus animé + mode Focus immersif avec sélection de section

- Bouton Focus : halo radial breathe, orbit spin avec dot, ripple au clic
- Mode Focus : overlay de sélection "Sur quoi veux-tu te concentrer ?"
  avec liste de toutes les sections par chapitre
- Vue immersive : fond assombri, section en grand avec hint/tips/sous-tâches
  cochables, compteur de temps de focus, Pomodoro auto-lancé
- FocusTimer : compteur mm:ss de temps écoulé depuis le début du focus
- Escape/Quitter : retour au dashboard, reset de tous les états focus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(skills): add Reddit skill + remove Chrome references from prompts

Chrome CDP tool was already disabled but skill files still mentioned it,
causing the bot to hallucinate about Chrome availability. Removed chrome.md
(backed up as .bak), cleaned web-search.md, and added reddit.md skill
using Reddit's JSON API via web_fetch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add better error messages for email sending failures

- Return Postmark error details from API for debugging
- Display actual error message in toast notification
- Add clearer message when POSTMARK_API_TOKEN is missing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: AI retry button gets token interactively and shows errors (#9)

* Stop AI retry loop on Google reauth failure

When runPendingAiRetry hits google_reauth_required, mark that account
in a reauthNeeded Set and skip all subsequent emails for it. This
prevents the retry loop from repeatedly overwriting reauthRequired=true
after the user reconnects.

Also don't advance nextRetryAt for Google auth failures so emails
stay immediately due for retry once the user reconnects Gmail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix reconnect loop: block auto-sync/retry during OAuth popup

The OAuth popup closing fires a window "focus" event which triggered
runAutoSync immediately. At that moment the emails closure was stale
(token:null, reauthRequired:true from before reconnect), causing a
google_reauth_required error that overwrote the reauthRequired:false
set by connectGoogleAccount.

Fix: add connectBusyRef (a mutable ref, always current unlike closure
state) that is set to "google"/"microsoft" before requestGoogleToken
is awaited and cleared in the finally block. runAutoSync checks this
ref at the top and bails out immediately, preventing any retry or
auto-sync from running while an OAuth popup is open.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: clear reauthRequired immediately on token receive, before profile call

The root cause of the endless reconnect loop: connectGoogleAccount calls
gmailGetProfile *after* getting the OAuth token but *before* setting
reauthRequired:false. If gmailGetProfile fails for any reason (network
blip, transient 401, API quota, etc.) the catch block runs and
reauthRequired:true is never cleared — even though the user successfully
authorized with Google.

Fix for reconnect (existing account):
- Immediately set reauthRequired:false + save token to sessionStorage
  as soon as we have the OAuth token, before any API calls
- gmailGetProfile is then called to update the email address but a
  failure there no longer affects the connected state

Also add connectBusyRef guard directly to runPendingAiRetry (not just
runAutoSync) so manual "Retry AI Pending" during OAuth is blocked too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: prevent stale sync/retry from overwriting reauthRequired after reconnect

The root cause: runPendingAiRetry and runSync are long-running async functions
that can start BEFORE the user clicks Reconnect. When the user reconnects
(setting reauthRequired:false), these already-running functions continue with
stale tokens. When they inevitably fail with 401/google_reauth_required, their
catch blocks unconditionally set reauthRequired:true — overwriting the fix.

Three-layer protection:
1. Break out of runPendingAiRetry loop if connectBusyRef becomes set mid-loop
2. Guard all reauthRequired:true writes with connectBusyRef check
3. Use setEmails updater to check lastAuthAt from LATEST state (not stale
   closure) — skip reauthRequired:true if account was authed < 30 seconds ago

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Change auto-sync interval from 60 minutes to 30 minutes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: only user-initiated actions can mark reauthRequired, never background

Root cause: background auto-sync and AI retry were setting reauthRequired:true
on ANY transient Google/Microsoft API hiccup. This made the connection appear
broken even when it was fine — a temporary 401 during a silent background sync
would immediately show "re-auth needed" to the user.

Architectural fix:
- runPendingAiRetry (always background): NEVER sets reauthRequired:true anymore.
  Just logs and skips. The proactive token refresh handles recovery silently.
- runSync with interactive:false/silent:true (auto-sync): same — never marks reauth.
- runSync with interactive:true (user clicked Sync): CAN set reauthRequired:true,
  but only if lastAuthAt is older than 55 minutes (token lifetime). Within that
  window, the token is assumed valid and transient errors are ignored.
- connectBusyRef guard remains as an additional safety layer.

Result: connection stays "Connected" permanently. Only a user-initiated sync
that fails AFTER the token lifetime has elapsed will surface "re-auth needed."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: AI retry button now gets token interactively and shows errors

When user clicks "Retry AI Pending" (force:true):
- ensureGoogleToken now uses interactive:true so it can open Google popup
  if silent token refresh fails — previously hardcoded to interactive:false,
  meaning retries silently failed when the token was expired
- Auth errors are shown visibly (log message + reauthRequired:true) so user
  knows to reconnect

Background retries (force:false) remain silent — no popups, no reauthRequired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-detect pH and temperature from PRIDE project metadata

- _detect_conditions(): extracts pH/pD and labelling temperature from
  sampleProcessingProtocol using targeted regex (prefers labelling/incubation
  context over chromatography temperatures)
- --ph and --temperature now default to None; resolved after metadata fetch:
    - no arg + detection succeeds  → use detected value (printed with source)
    - no arg + detection fails     → fallback default (7.5 / 25°C) + warning
    - user arg + detected differs  → use user value + mismatch warning
    - user arg + detected matches  → use user value silently
- _print_project_summary() no longer duplicates condition hints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: revert MiniMax model from M1 back to M2.5

M1 reasoning model consumes more tokens (reasoning tokens count against
TPM), contributing to rate limit issues. M2.5 is the intended model.

commit.message:
feat(ci): unified coverage report merging unit + story tests (#105)

* feat(ci): unified coverage report merging unit + story tests

- Update test and storybook-tests CI jobs to output blob reports
- Add coverage-report CI job that merges blobs and posts combined report
- Remove Post coverage report from test job (now in coverage-report)
- Remove @storybook/addon-coverage (replaced by unified Vitest coverage)
- Add .vitest-reports to .gitignore

Closes #96

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle vitest browser mode hang in storybook tests CI

Vitest browser mode doesn't exit cleanly after tests complete. Use
timeout with fallback and verify the blob file was written.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add artifact pattern filter to coverage-report download step

The download-artifact action needs a pattern to match the specific
vitest-blob-* artifacts, otherwise it fails to find the reports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* debug: remove --outputFile flag, add ls debug step for blob reports

Testing if Vitest 4 blob reporter outputs to a different location
than what --outputFile specifies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use non-hidden vitest-reports dir for artifact upload

The .vitest-reports/ hidden directory was not being found by
actions/upload-artifact@v4. Switch to vitest-reports/ (no dot prefix)
for blob report output and artifact upload/download paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ignore exit code from merge-reports step

The --merge-reports replays test results from blob data, and storybook
tests fail during replay because no browser is available. The coverage
data is still correctly merged despite the non-zero exit code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct prompt input line wrapping in task form (#90)

The `max_prompt_display` calculation double-counted 2 border rows:
`non_prompt_rows` included border rows for panel height computation,
but was then subtracted from `inner.height` which already excludes
borders. This made the prompt display area always 2 lines shorter
than the actual content, causing text to scroll/disappear prematurely
when wrapping to multiple lines.

Introduced in 08a826c.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: add .gitignore, exclude .claude/ dev config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(web): reduce ESLint warnings from 9 to 8 through major refactoring

Refactored 5 largest components/hooks to eliminate max-lines-per-function violations:

## 1. UserSettingsModal.tsx (857 lines → 119 lines, 86% reduction)
Extracted into:
- 4 custom hooks: useUserProfile, useAvatarUpload, useProfileUpdate, useModalClickOutside
- 5 UI components: LoadingState, ErrorState, Header, FormFields, Footer
- 1 styles file: styles.ts
Main component: 735 lines → 99 lines ✅

## 2. useChat.ts (394 lines → 159 lines, 60% reduction)
Extracted into 7 specialized hooks:
- useChatHistoryLoader.ts - History loading and sync
- useChatPagination.ts - Pagination logic
- useChatStreamSync.ts - WebSocket stream sync
- useChatEventHandlers.ts - Event listeners
- useChatReconnection.ts - Reconnection tracking
- useChatSendHandler.ts - Message sending
- useChatModels.ts - Model fetching
Main hook: 394 lines → 159 lines (still 2 violations but major improvement)

## 3. SidebarSessionList.tsx (374 lines → 123 lines, 67% reduction)
Extracted into:

[cdc] Add metadata column support for Kafka CDC connector (#7315)

This PR supercedes https://github.com/apache/paimon/pull/6353 - I took
over this work from my colleague @gmdfalk

## Description
Add --metadata_column support to Paimon Kafka CDC connector, similar to
the already existing options added for MySQL and Postgres:
https://github.com/apache/paimon/pull/2077
Also add optional --metadata_column_prefix to avoid conflicts with
existing Paimon fields like topic, timestamp etc.

Supported metadata columns are those on
org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRecord i.e.:

- topic
- partition
- offset
- timestamp
- timestampType: This is the name of the enum i.e. NoTimestampType,
CreateTime or LogAppendTime

The feature is backwards compatible. It's only active when
--metadata_column is supplied resp.
SynchronizationActionBase.withMetadataColumns is used.

## Motivation
This is a requested feature:
https://github.com/apache/paimon/issues/3210

We primarly use this feature for two purposes:

1. Troubleshooting and data lineage (e.g. where in our Kafka
infrastructure does this Paimon row come from?)
2. Mapping large Kafka topic partitions 1:1 to Paimon buckets to avoid
reshuffling (see this issue it would solve:
https://github.com/apache/paimon/issues/3249)

## Tests
Unit and Integration Tests

## API and Format
No changes to public apis or storage format.

The changes here are contained to the flink cdc package but I did have
to update CdcSourceRecord since it previously didn't provide a way to
surface arbitrary metadata for a record.

The metadata attribute on CdcSourceRecord is intentionally a generic Map
so that it can potentially be used to add metadata support for other
connectors like Pulsar or Mongo that are not yet implemented.

## Documentation
Added the new --metadata_column and --metadata_column_prefix parameter
to Kafka CDC docs.

## Dev notes
For running integration tests on MacOS with Rancher Desktop, i had to
properly expose the docker socket to testcontainers e.g. system wide via
sudo ln -sf "$HOME/.rd/docker.sock" /var/run/docker.sock.

---------

Signed-off-by: Max Falk <gfalk@yelp.com>
Co-authored-by: Max Falk <gfalk@yelp.com>
Co-authored-by: Max Falk <279131+gmdfalk@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Rork duplicate notifications by hashing raw content instead of AI summary

The sourceURL (Rork) case was computing SHA256 from Gemini's summary output,
which is non-deterministic. Even with identical page content, Gemini could
produce slightly different summaries, causing the hash to change and triggering
duplicate notifications. Now hashes the raw Jina markdown content instead,
and only calls Gemini for summarization after confirming the content has changed.

Also fix .gitignore to not ignore cmd/news/ directory (only the root binary).

Closes #8
